company_values_and_culture: Passionate about beautiful API design and developer experience
High bar for code quality and engineering standards
Big-picture thinking balancing technical work with business impact
analysis.domain_context: Duffel is rebuilding travel infrastructure by building APIs that simplify airline booking and travel distribution across hundreds of providers.

research.what_company_does: Duffel rebuilds travel infrastructure by providing APIs that simplify airline booking and travel distribution across hundreds of providers. They enable developers and travel platforms to integrate complex flight, hotel, and car booking seamlessly.
concrete_hooks_for_cover_letter: Reference their focus on beautiful API design and developer experienceâshow how you've prioritized clarity and usability in past backend work, not just functionality
Mention the scale and complexity of integrating hundreds of travel providers, positioning yourself as someone who thrives solving distributed system challenges
Acknowledge their Y Combinator backing and early-stage impact modelâdemonstrate you're energized by rapid iteration and measurable business outcomes, not just technical depth
values_to_mirror: Meticulous about code quality and engineering standards (not cutting corners)
Developer-centric thinking (building for the next engineer, not just the machine)
Clear communication and structured articulation (over assumed knowledge)
Balance between technical excellence and pragmatic business impact
caution_notes: Don't overclaim travel domain expertise if you lack itânice-to-have, not required; focus on transferable API integration patterns instead
Avoid vague statements like 'I love building things'âbe specific about API design decisions or code quality wins
Don't position yourself as a lone wolfâemphasize collaboration and feedback loops, as the role signals structured communication is core
Don't imply you need extensive mentoring or stable processesâearly-stage Y Combinator culture values independence and adaptability

summary: Full-stack backend engineer with 2 years of production Spring Boot microservices experience designing REST APIs and optimising system performanceâcut API response time by 25%, query execution time by 30%, and system downtime by 15% through structured debugging and root-cause analysis. Shipped RideX, a live full-stack ride-hailing platform integrating three production third-party APIs (Stripe, Mapbox, Firebase) with role-based access control, persistent cloud data, and real-time synchronisation across distributed components. Currently studying distributed systems, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure through an AWS-accredited MSc at University of East London, with hands-on DevOps and CI/CD pipeline experience in Jenkins and GitHub Actions.

coverLetter:
Dear Duffel Team,

I'm drawn to your mission of rebuilding travel infrastructure through elegant APIs because I've seen firsthand how thoughtful backend design unlocks value downstream. At Brane Group, I cut API response time by 25% on a high-volume Spring Boot transaction serviceânot through brute-force optimization, but by diagnosing the root cause, refactoring the query path, and shipping a solution that made the entire system faster and more predictable for consumers. That same discipline around clarity, measurement, and developer experience is what I'd bring to scaling Duffel's distributed booking infrastructure across hundreds of providers.

**API Design and Backend Development:** I've designed and shipped REST APIs in production, owned end-to-end optimization from diagnosis through deployment, and debugged real third-party integration edge cases (Stripe, Mapbox, Firebase webhooks, auth token lifecycles). I understand that great API design is inseparable from great observabilityâI built monitoring and logging practices that caught bottlenecks before they hit users.

**System Integration and Distributed Systems Thinking:** My RideX project required orchestrating three production third-party APIs with real-time synchronization, role-based access control, and persistent state across Firebase Firestore and Realtime Database. I've also optimized PostgreSQL and MySQL schemas and indexing strategies at scale, reducing query execution time by 30%âthe kind of systems thinking that translates directly to coordinating hundreds of travel providers.

**Code Quality and Engineering Standards:** I mentored 3 junior developers through structured code review, reducing recurring defects by 20%, and built CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions) that cut deployment time by 40%. I don't treat code quality as overhead; it's how teams move fast without breaking things. I'm currently studying Kubernetes and distributed systems as part of my MSc, so I'm actively building the foundations to grow into more complex infrastructure work.

Your Y Combinator backed approach to rapid iteration and measurable impact resonates with me. I thrive in environments where technical rigour serves clear business outcomesâwhere a 25% latency win or a 30% query optimization is valued because it compounds into user trust and platform reliability.

I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my backend experience and API-first thinking can help Duffel move faster while maintaining the engineering standards that make your platform a joy to integrate against.

Best regards,  
Soma Shekar Keesari  
+44 7553 449836  
somashekarkeesari18@gmail.com

recommendations: Add a single line to the summary acknowledging the tech stack mismatch honestly: 'Currently learning Elixir and Phoenix through [specific resource/project]' â or remove it if not true. Duffel will respect honesty over false claims.

Explicitly name 'Google Cloud Platform' once in the skills section under Cloud & DevOps alongside AWS, even if experience is minimal â search algorithms will match the keyword without overstating depth.

Strengthen the 'travel technology' hit by reframing one sentence in projects: change 'comparable to the systematic data integration Duffel performs across travel provider APIs' to explicitly state 'travel industry context' or add a line about interest in travel/booking domain.
atsScore.overall_assessment: This CV is **borderline submittable but at a disadvantage**. You have solid coverage of API design, backend development, and system integration â Duffel's core needs â but the missing Elixir/Phoenix and GCP stack are red flags for a role explicitly built around those technologies. The CV reads strong on Java/AWS and demonstrates real shipping discipline, but Duffel's tech stack is non-negotiable. **Competitive position: Pass screening on general backend strength, but lose to candidates with Elixir/Phoenix experience in the technical interview phase.** Only submit if you can credibly claim active Elixir learning or if the role description signals flexibility on stack.
